title: SQL: wrong results of shift with type casting
poc: select cast(98 as bigint) << cast (32 as bigint);
select cast(1 as bigint) << cast (32 as bigint);

SELECT 1<<7 properly produces nil (=-128) because the default type is bte.
 SELECT cast(i as int) <<7 produces 128.;
key_bug_features: Shift operation with type casting produces incorrect results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_shift (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value BIGINT NOT NULL);
SELECT value << 6 FROM test_shift WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT CAST(value AS BIGINT) << 8 FROM test_shift;
SELECT CAST(NULL AS BIGINT) << 32 FROM test_shift;
